goal: resolve friendly presentation metadata for every scalar row through a reference-matched adoption layer while keeping the backend, parser, validation, classification, proof system, and every save gate unchanged
matchSource: the existing normalized key mapping in data/reports/hyprmod-full-scalar-model-comparison.v0.55.2.json (colon-form reference keys normalized against official dotted setting keys); provenance for every adopted label is recorded in src/presentation_labels.rs as reference_key and verified by test
descriptionPolicy: descriptions are NOT copied from the reference app: the reference is GPL-3.0-licensed third-party work and this repository is public, so transplanting its authored prose without attribution is not acceptable; instead every row renders a one-line description derived from this project's own bundled official Hyprland metadata in the same simple user-facing style (ux_presentation::short_description). Verbatim description adoption remains available if the project owner decides to add the required license attribution - a licensing decision recorded for the owner, not taken silently
labelPolicy: adopted labels are short factual setting names (e.g. the blur/gaps/border option names); rows keep their internal row id and raw official Hyprland setting key unchanged
unmatchedPolicy: unmatched rows keep their official metadata label unchanged - nothing is guessed or invented; future humanized overrides would be a separate reviewed change
ambiguousPolicy: the normalized mapping produced no one-to-many matches (0 partial matches in the source comparison)
rejectedDetail: 3 reference-side options have no counterpart in the 341-row model (unmatchedHyprModRows in the source comparison) and were not force-fitted
dropdownLabelAdoption: finite-choice dropdowns now show a friendly display form (generic humanization: separators to spaces, capitalized) while the raw value is what gets applied, validated, and saved - raw values unchanged for every choice; rows whose model already carried a distinct label keep it
categoryGroupAdoption: the grouped sidebar categories (Look & Feel / Input / Display / Window Management / Advanced) follow the task-oriented grouping adopted in the UX simplification pass; rows keep their model-assigned tabs and subsections
rowsPresentationChanged: 127 list/detail labels + all finite-choice dropdown display labels + one-line descriptions on every row
additionalUxAdoptedThisSlice: hidden search: the entry hides by default, Ctrl+F reveals and focuses it, Escape (in or out of the entry) clears and hides it; categories stay the primary navigation
record picker card retitled to 'Animations & curves' with a two-line friendly intro; the proof explanation moved behind the Safety Details pointer and a quiet gate chip line
detail pane heading uses the resolved friendly label
deferredToNextSlice: color swatch + picker window for the 22 color entries (validation-preserving accept/cancel), gradient row controls
search index over label + description + raw key + category (today the existing search filters by the model's search projection)
detail-pane long-text collapse behind Details expanders
structuredFamilies: presentation only: hl.animation/hl.curve keep the proven picker surfaces (no expansion); hl.monitor/hl.bind/hl.device/hl.permission/hl.gesture stay blocked or review-only on the Safety Details page; no creation/deletion; style editing stays blocked
verification: tests/hyprmod_presentation_adoption.rs pins the counts, the provenance mapping, raw-key preservation, unchanged classification counts, dropdown raw-value preservation, the Ctrl+F wiring, and the absence of proof-wall text on the Config page
